title: Tanker report - Week 28 2016
summary:
VLCCs
Despite a good volume of fixing, rates have remained steady with 270,000 tonnes going long east hovering in the low to mid WS 40s with small discounts available for either new building, ex-drydock or tonnage over 15 years. Going west, rates for 280,000 tonnes to the US Gulf have been largely unchanged in the WS 25/27 region basis cape/cape routing. In West Africa, rates softened further losing 1 to 1.5 points with China runs being covered at WS 47.5/48.25 level.

children.title: VLCCs
children.summary: Despite a good volume of fixing, rates have remained steady with 270,000 tonnes going long east hovering in the low to mid WS 40s with small discounts available for either new building, ex-drydock or tonnage over 15 years. Going west, rates for 280,000 tonnes to the US Gulf have been largely unchanged in the WS 25/27 region basis cape/cape routing.

children.title: Suezmaxes
children.summary: In West Africa, rates have come under renewed downward pressure. Reduced supply of Nigerian crude, combined with VLCCs being very competitive, has left suezmax owners facing levels of barely WS 60 for 130,000 tonnes to Europe.

children.title: Aframaxes
children.summary: In the Mediterranean, the market has continued to drop as a healthy tonnage list takes its toll. Petrogal covered an attractive voyage from Sidi Kerir to Portugal at WS 73 while a short Sidi Kerir/Italy run is understood to have been fixed at WS 79.75.

children.title: Panamaxes
children.summary: On the Continent, it has been a difficult week for owners. Cargoes from both Brofjorden and the Baltic failed at WS 95 for transatlantic.

children.title: Clean
children.summary:
In the 37,000 tonnes Cont/USAC trade, there has been a healthy level of enquiry but with plenty of tonnage around, rates here have been stuck at WS 90, while in the US Gulf the 38,000 tonnes back haul market remains weak, albeit steady with rates holding at around WS 70. For daily tanker market assessments from the Baltic Exchange please visit www.balticexchange.com/market-information/
